jygame Repository
jygame Documentation

Appearance

JyLearnUnderstand game engines from the inside out

A progressive educational resource that teaches game engine architecture, memory management, rendering pipelines, and particle systems using jygame as its real-world case study.

Start Learning
Explore the Architecture
jygame on GitHub

Progressive Curriculum

12 parts, 90+ chapters. Start with zero knowledge and build complete understanding. Every concept taught before it is used.

Real-World Case Study

jygame is not a toy. Every concept maps to actual engine code with complete walkthroughs of Pool, ActivePool, SoAParticleStorage, CPU and GPU backends, WGSL shaders, and WASM SIMD.

Architecture Deep Dive

Understand not just how code works, but why it was designed that way. Explore data-oriented design, cache locality, GPU compute pipelines, and modifier composition.

Memory to GPU

Start with stack vs heap. End with WebGPU compute shaders and SIMD-accelerated death sweeps. The full stack, taught layer by layer.

Multiple Audiences

Beginners learn programming concepts. Game developers study architecture patterns. Performance engineers explore SIMD, SoA, and GPU compute. All in one resource.

Beyond One Engine

The concepts taught here apply to any engine or game. jygame is the vehicle, not the destination. You will understand how modern game engines work.
